tool_calls.name: execute_lisp

{"code":"(let [issues (linear_issues {:state "in_progress"})
      grouped (group-by :assignee issues)
      counts (into {} (for [[assignee issues-list] grouped]
                        [assignee (count issues-list)]))]
  (apply max-key val counts))"}

text: Let me use a different approach with Clojure's built-in functions.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"(let [issues (linear_issues {:state "in_progress"})
      grouped (group-by :assignee issues)]
  (reduce (fn [max-entry [assignee issues-list]]
            (let [count (count issues-list)]
              (if (or (nil? max-entry) (> count (second max-entry)))
                [assignee count]
                max-entry)))
          nil
          grouped))"}
